description: You are an AV technician for a natural history museum installing a 3-screen video wall using three 1080x1920 portrait monitors mounted side-by-side. Slice the master panoramic video located at /home/ga/Videos/signage/panorama_master.mp4 (3240x1920) into three portrait videos corresponding to the Left (X=0 to 1080), Center (X=1080 to 2160), and Right (X=2160 to 3240) thirds of the original frame. Remove the audio tracks from all three slices and export them as H.264 MP4 files to /home/ga/Videos/signage/wall_slices/ with the names 'screen_1_left.mp4', 'screen_2_center.mp4', and 'screen_3_right.mp4'. In the same directory, create an infinitely looping VLC XSPF playlist named 'wall_test_playlist.xspf' containing the three slices. Additionally, generate a JSON manifest named 'signage_manifest.json' in that directory containing an 'exhibit' key ('Panoramic Earth'), a 'master_resolution' key ('3240x1920'), and a 'panels' array documenting the 'filename', 'resolution', and 'audio_stripped' (boolean) status for each of the three panels.
